details: The scheme “Pradhan Mantri Matsya Sampada Yojana (PMMSY)” with the component of "Refrigerated vehicles", introduced by the Agriculture, Farmers Welfare and Co-operation Department, Government of Gujarat, aims to modernise cold chain infrastructure to preserve fish health, uplift fishermen, and enhance value chain efficiency. Through this scheme, financial support is provided to fishermen and related beneficiaries for constructing ice plants or storage units with a minimum capacity of 30 tonnes, ensuring hygienic fish storage and better returns. The scheme is implemented by the Commissioner of Fisheries of the State Governments, with 60% contribution from the Government of India and 40% contribution from the State Government.

Marine production in the Gujarat state is one of the leading in the country. The state lags behind in exports due to non-norms. So cold storage/ice plant modernization is necessary to keep the fish in healthy condition.

Insulated vehicle, three wheeler with ice box including e-rickshaw, and live fish vending center to deliver the fish in hygienic condition to the consumer, so that the consumer (customer) can get fresh and clean fish from the local area and get good returns to the sellers.

Around 27,000 small and large boats are operating in the state. To strengthen the safety and security of the fishermen and increase the income of the boat owner, replacement of the existing boats is necessary to increase the sea growth of the existing boats.
benefits: The unit cost of a refrigerated vehicle is ₹25,00,000/-.
40% assistance (up to ₹10,00,000/-) is provided for general category beneficiaries.
60% assistance (up to ₹15,00,000/-) is provided for women and SC/ST category beneficiaries.
The benefit will be available as per the target (category) allocated by the Government of India.
The benefit is available once in a lifetime.
Contribution is shared: 60% by the Government of India and 40% by State Governments.
Once in a lifetime for eligible beneficiaries.
eligibility: The applicant should be a native of Gujarat state.
The applicant should have a fishing boat registered under Real Craft software.
The applicant should hold a fishing license.
The applicant should complete the online registration on the
The applicant should obtain in-principle approval before purchase of the floor plan.
The applicant should have his/her own bank account.
Women, SC, and ST beneficiaries are eligible for 60% assistance of the unit cost.
General category beneficiaries are eligible for 40% assistance of the unit cost.

online: The applicant may visit and apply online at the
Click on 'Login' from the top right-hand side, then click on "Beneficiary Registration" and select any one from the 'Farmer' or 'Institutional' or 'Except For the Farmer' as the Beneficiary Type.

Enter the 'Company Name' and Address if you have selected 'Institutional' or 'Except For the Farmer' as the Beneficiary Type. And for all select the District, Taluka, and Village, choose 'what type of account holder you are?', enter your Account Number and Name, solve the Captcha, and click 'Save and Next'.
Enter your Mobile Number and E-mail address, click 'Send OTP', enter the received OTP, create and confirm a Password, solve the Captcha, and click 'Submit'.
Click on 'Login' from the top right-hand side of the homepage. On the home page, click on ‘Schemes’ and then click on ‘Agricultural Schemes’.
Now select the scheme, click on the ‘Apply’ button, and an application page will open.
Click on the "Apply New" button and submit a new application.
Click on the "Update Application" button to add corrections to the application.
Once the application is done, confirm it.
Take a printout of the confirmed application.

faqs.question: What is the unit cost of a refrigerated vehicle under this scheme?
faqs.answer: The unit cost of a refrigerated vehicle is ₹25,00,000/-.

faqs.question: How much financial assistance is given to general category beneficiaries?
faqs.answer: General category beneficiaries receive 40% assistance, up to ₹10,00,000/-.

faqs.question: How much assistance is provided to women and SC/ST category beneficiaries?
faqs.answer: Women and SC/ST category beneficiaries receive 60% assistance, up to ₹15,00,000/-.

faqs.question: Is there any difference in assistance based on category?
faqs.answer: Yes, general category beneficiaries get 40% assistance, while women and SC/ST category beneficiaries get 60% assistance.

faqs.question: How is the financial contribution shared between governments?
faqs.answer: The Government of India provides 60% contribution, and the State Governments provide 40% contribution.

faqs.question: Is the benefit available multiple times for a beneficiary?
faqs.answer: No, the benefit can be availed of only once in a lifetime.

faqs.question: Will all applicants automatically receive the benefit?
faqs.answer: No, the benefit is available as per the target (category) allocated by the Government of India.

faqs.question: Who decides the allocation of targets for assistance?
faqs.answer: The allocation of targets is done by the Government of India.

faqs.question: What is the maximum financial assistance a beneficiary can get?
faqs.answer: A beneficiary can receive up to ₹15,00,000/- as financial assistance.

faqs.question: Can a general category beneficiary get the same level of assistance as women or SC/ST beneficiaries?
faqs.answer: No, general category beneficiaries get up to ₹10,00,000/-, while women and SC/ST beneficiaries can get up to ₹15,00,000/-.
